name: Amadeus Travel API
what_it_does: Global Distribution System (GDS) API for flight search, hotel search, seat maps, travel recommendations, and airline data. One of the world's largest travel content APIs with real-time availability and pricing.
use_cases: Building AI-powered flight search and comparison agents
Hotel search and availability checking for travel booking workflows
Flight price predictions and fare analysis
Seat map display and seat selection in booking flows
Travel inspiration and destination recommendation engines
not_for: Instant confirmed bookings without IATA accreditation (search is easier than booking)
Consumer applications without proper licensing and compliance
Very small travel agencies (complex IATA/GDS requirements)
best_when: An agent needs comprehensive flight and hotel search with real-time pricing and availability from a GDS, especially for building travel assistant or booking tools.
avoid_when: You need simple flight status or basic itinerary data — Amadeus is feature-rich but complex to implement correctly.

auth.notes: OAuth2 client credentials flow for server-side (agent) use. API key + secret exchanged for Bearer token. Token expires in 30 minutes and must be refreshed. Simple for agent automation — no user consent needed.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Test environment free; production requires registration and volume-based pricing
pricing.paid_tiers: Pay-per-call in production; varies by API — typically $0.0001-$0.01 per call
pricing.notes: Free test environment with cached/simulated data is excellent for development. Production requires moving to live environment with commercial agreement. Self-service production access available for small volumes.

performance.rate_limits: 2,000 calls/month free test; production limits by commercial tier

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Errors include code, title, detail, and source fields. Airline/GDS-specific error codes can be opaque. IATA error codes require domain knowledge to interpret. Documentation helps but GDS concepts are complex.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Search endpoints are read-only GET requests — fully idempotent. Booking endpoints (production) require careful idempotency handling.
known_agent_gotchas: Test environment uses cached/simulated data — production may behave differently
Flight offers expire quickly (minutes) — agents must book promptly after search
IATA codes required for airports/airlines — agents need lookup table or validation step
Flight search response contains offer IDs that must be used verbatim in booking calls
Cabin class codes (Y, M, W, C, F) are IATA-specific — not human-readable
GDS flight data has complex pricing rules and fare conditions that require parsing
OAuth2 30-minute token expiry requires proactive refresh in long-running agents

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth2 client credentials. Separate test and production environments. Travel data involves PII. GDPR considerations for passenger data. PCI DSS relevant for payment flows.
